nodes.description: Abstract specification of a communication protocol defining message formats, lifecycle semantics, capability discovery, and interoperability rules between autonomous participants.
nodes.description_zh: 定义消息格式、生命周期语义、能力发现和自主参与者之间的互操作性规则的通信协议的抽象规范。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 协议根

version.description: Semantic version of the protocol specification

spec_uri.description: Canonical URI to normative specification document

message_format.description: Primary message serialization and framing style

nodes.label: Protocol Endpoint
nodes.description: Addressable service point that implements one or more protocol roles, exposing capabilities, accepting messages, and participating in task lifecycles.
nodes.description_zh: 可寻址服务点，实现一个或多个协议角色、公开功能、接受消息以及参与任务生命周期。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 端点平面 / 传输通道

supported_roles.description: Protocol roles this endpoint can assume

nodes.label: Protocol Role
nodes.description: Role a participant plays in a protocol conversation such as client, server, peer, or observer, determining allowed operations and message flow direction.
nodes.description_zh: 参与者在协议对话中扮演的角色（例如客户端、服务器、对等方或观察者）确定允许的操作和消息流方向。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 端点平面 / 传输通道

role_name.description: Named role in the protocol interaction

initiates_connection.description: Whether this role may initiate connections

may_delegate_tasks.description: Whether this role can submit delegated work

nodes.label: Resource Exposure
nodes.description: A data resource exposed through a protocol for read or subscription, identified by URI and typed with MIME or schema metadata.
nodes.description_zh: 通过协议公开以供读取或订阅的数据资源，由 URI 标识并使用 MIME 或架构元数据键入。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 授权平面 / 能力平面

nodes.label: Streaming Channel
nodes.description: Real-time unidirectional or bidirectional data stream between endpoints for progress updates, partial results, or continuous event delivery.
nodes.description_zh: 端点之间的实时单向或双向数据流，用于进度更新、部分结果或连续事件传递。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 端点平面 / 传输通道

event_types.description: Event types deliverable on this channel

nodes.label: Task Envelope
nodes.description: Structured wrapper encapsulating a delegated task including identity, input payload, metadata, correlation identifiers, and delivery expectations.
nodes.description_zh: 结构化包装器封装委托任务，包括身份、输入有效负载、元数据、相关标识符和交付期望。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 任务平面 / 消息平面

nodes.label: Task Lifecycle
nodes.description: Protocol-level state machine governing delegated task progression from submission through completion, failure, or cancellation.
nodes.description_zh: 协议级状态机管理委派任务从提交到完成、失败或取消的进程。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 任务平面 / 消息平面

supports_input_needed.description: Whether protocol supports interactive input requests

nodes.label: Tool Exposure
nodes.description: A callable tool made available through a protocol endpoint, including input schema, output schema, and invocation semantics.
nodes.description_zh: 通过协议端点提供的可调用工具，包括输入模式、输出模式和调用语义。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 授权平面 / 能力平面

input_schema_ref.description: Reference to input parameter schema definition

destructive.description: Whether invocation may cause irreversible side effects

nodes.label: Transport
nodes.description: Communication channel mechanism carrying protocol messages between endpoints, such as stdio, HTTP, WebSocket, or message queue bindings.
nodes.description_zh: 在端点之间承载协议消息的通信通道机制，例如 stdio、HTTP、WebSocket 或消息队列绑定。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 端点平面 / 传输通道

max_message_size.description: Maximum message size in bytes if bounded

nodes.label: Adapter API
nodes.description: API for registering and connecting environment, model, tool, and storage adapters to the SDK kernel.
nodes.description_zh: 用于将环境、模型、工具和存储适配器注册并连接到 SDK 内核的 API。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 适配器平面

api_version.description: Semantic version of the API surface

nodes.label: Agent Definition API
nodes.description: API for declaring agent identity, role, capabilities, system instructions, and behavioral constraints at configuration time.
nodes.description_zh: 用于在配置时声明智能体身份、角色、功能、系统指令和行为约束的 API。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 核心 API 表面 / SDK 产品表面 / 领域 API 表面

api_version.description: Semantic version of the API surface

nodes.label: Agent SDK
nodes.description: Top-level software development kit abstraction providing a unified surface for building, configuring, and running autonomous agents without vendor lock-in.
nodes.description_zh: 顶级软件开发工具包抽象提供了一个统一的界面，用于构建、配置和运行自主智能体，而无需锁定供应商。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 核心 API 表面 / SDK 产品表面 / 领域 API 表面

nodes.label: Callback
nodes.description: User-provided function registered at a hook point and invoked when the corresponding lifecycle event occurs.
nodes.description_zh: 用户提供的函数在钩子点注册并在相应的生命周期事件发生时调用。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 扩展平面 / 运行时模型

priority.description: Invocation priority relative to other callbacks on same hook

nodes.label: Connector
nodes.description: Adapter component connecting the SDK to an external service, protocol endpoint, or data source with normalized interface semantics.
nodes.description_zh: 适配器组件使用标准化接口语义将 SDK 连接到外部服务、协议端点或数据源。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 适配器平面

target_service.description: External service or protocol being connected

nodes.label: SDK Environment Adapter
nodes.description: SDK-side adapter registration object that binds the generic AdapterAPI to concrete environment adapters without owning the environment ontology concept.
nodes.description_zh: SDK端适配器注册对象，将通用AdapterAPI绑定到具体环境适配器，而不拥有环境本体概念。
nodes.intra_axis_zh: 适配器平面

nodes.label: Error Model
nodes.description: Structured error type hierarchy defining error codes, categories, retry semantics, and propagation rules across SDK layers.

edges.description: Assigns schema artifacts to a namespace for id prefixing, ownership, and extension isolation.
Links schema artifacts to graph constraints that must hold for valid ontology instances at validation time.
Structural containment: an ontology graph contains subgraphs; a subgraph contains its node classes, edge classes, constraints, and views.
Ownership edge indicating which subgraph module defines a given schema artifact. Enables modular validation and release boundaries.
Provenance edge connecting ontology constructs to external evidence references documenting their theoretical or practical basis.
Associates a typed attribute specification with a node or edge class definition, forming the attribute schema of the class.
Declares that instances of a node class must satisfy one or more interface contracts, enabling polymorphic validation across subgraphs.
Binds an operational or lifecycle state machine to a node class whose instances transition through declared states.
Records version history of the ontology graph. Each release is a Version node linked from the root OntologyGraph.
Constrains agent operation within a trust and permission envelope including autonomy level and escalation rules.
Hierarchical decomposition from goals or objectives into executable tasks assigned to agents or subagents.
Declares capabilities available to the agent, subject to boundary and policy constraints at invocation time.
Binds an agent instance to its stable identity record for discovery, delegation, and audit attribution.
Associates layered behavioral instructions with the agent, prepended to each decision loop context assembly.
Assigns one or more functional roles to an agent, optionally combined with persona for multi-agent orchestration.
Declares goals the agent is actively pursuing. Goal-driven behavior is a foundational contract for all agent instances.
Agent ingests feedback signals from tools, humans, evaluators, or environment to adapt subsequent loop iterations.
Binds the agent to its active decision loop instance governing perceive- reason-act iterations.
Each decision loop iteration mutates agent state with new observations, plan progress, and intermediate results.
Connects cognitive and decision components to the model invocation abstraction handling request/response protocols.
Text segmentation producing bounded output or context chunks according to chunk policy rules.
Progressive disclosure mechanism triggering full content load from indexed summary upon query, selection, or tool use.
Content or metadata inserted into sparse, dense, or graph indexes for later retrieval and rank fusion.
Context assembly pulls content from registered sources within container boundaries and trust policies.
Context assembly ranks sources and segments by relevance, recency, or policy precedence when token budget is constrained.
Context sources and assemblies deliver material to the working context graph or directly to the agent cognitive input.
Context elements cite or link to underlying sources for provenance, re-fetch, and information flow tracking.
Full content or metadata compressed into light index entries for discovery without loading complete source material.
Content exceeding length limits is truncated according to chunk policy including head-tail preservation strategies.
A memory compactor produces summarized or merged memory records from source records.
A raw chunk is contextualized into a contextualized chunk by augmenting it with situating context summary.
Text chunks are converted to dense vectors using an embedding model.
A forgetting policy governs removal or archival of memory records according to defined rules.
Rank fusion combines score lists from multiple retrieval indexes into a unified ranking.
Retrieved or reconstructed memory content is injected into the active working memory context window.
A reranker refines the relevance ordering of candidate memory records after initial retrieval.
A memory retriever queries and reads records from a memory store as part of the retrieval pipeline.
A sparse index maintains lexical lookup structures over text chunks for keyword retrieval.
A memory store persistently holds one or more memory records, forming the primary containment relationship in the storage layer.
A vector index accelerates similarity search over vectors held in a vector store.
A memory writer persists new or updated records into a memory store.
Reasoning and planning activities consume resources from an allocated thinking budget.
A confidence estimate quantifies certainty about a thought, step, or entire plan.
A branch evaluator scores or assesses candidate thought nodes in the search space.
A reasoning process expands the search space by generating new thought nodes.
A planning process produces a structured plan as its output.
Reflection performs self-assessment on a reasoning trace, plan, or specific step.
A replanning trigger causes revision or replacement of an existing plan.
A branch evaluator selects a preferred thought node for further expansion.
A reasoning or planning process terminates when a stopping condition is satisfied.
Network actions initiate HTTP, socket, or API communication with external services.
A tool definition includes a formal schema describing its input and output structure.
A tool definition is surfaced through a tool discovery process.
Programmatic tool calling executes generated orchestration code in a sandbox.
An external action produces one or more observable side effects in the environment.
A tool call requests invocation of a specific tool definition.
A tool definition is matched as a candidate by a tool search query.
Computer use and GUI actions operate on graphical interface elements.
A failed tool invocation raises a tool error describing the failure.
File actions perform read or write operations on the file system. READS_FILE and WRITES_FILE are distinguished by the operation attribute on the file action node.
A successful tool invocation produces a structured tool result.
A tool definition is chosen by a tool selection decision for invocation.
A tool invocation may emit non-fatal warnings alongside or instead of full success.
Sequential composition linking one processing step directly to its successor in a fixed pipeline.
An orchestrator manages multi-agent or multi-branch coordination structures.
Assigns a subtask and scoped authority from a coordinator to a worker or subagent.
Initiates concurrent branch execution from a parallelization coordinator to independent subtasks.
A gate controls traversal between workflow steps, evaluating conditions before permitting progression.
Transfers control and context payload from one agent role to another.
Aggregates completed branch results back into the parallelization join point.
Links evaluator output to an optimizer that iteratively improves the assessed artifact.
An assessment relationship where an evaluator or reviewer examines an artifact or output.
Connects a router to one of its classification branches, binding a match label to a downstream target.
Participants publish and read shared coordination state through a blackboard.
A voting pattern selects among candidate outputs using evaluator scores or consensus rules.
Progresses a run to its next request-response turn.
Appends turn messages and tool results to the session transcript.
Enforces resource, token, or cost limits on a run or session.
Terminates in-progress execution via cancellation mechanism.
Persists a state snapshot to durable storage.
Records a discrete timeline occurrence during execution.
Nested execution containment from harness through sandbox to command.
Applies recovery strategy after failure using checkpoint or replay.
Flushes ephemeral context while anchoring preserved state to a continuation reference.
Rehydrates a resumable continuation point from stored checkpoint data.
Applies retry rules to a failed operation or execution.
Initiates a child lifecycle entity from a parent runtime container.
Captures process output streams for agent consumption.
Associates structured observability spans with a run.
Security-relevant decisions and proxy invocations are recorded immutably.
Policy evaluation yields an explicit permit decision with scoped conditions.
Policy evaluation yields an explicit block decision with rationale.
A blocked or ambiguous decision is elevated to higher authority for resolution.
A policy contract protects operations by attaching enforcement rules at guard points.
Information flow control assigns confidentiality and integrity labels to data artifacts.
Untrusted data taint marks propagate through processing per flow rules.
Credential proxy injects short-lived tokens without exposing secrets to untrusted zones.
An action or decision must receive human assessment before proceeding.
A rollback reverses a prior permitted action and records the reversal in audit.
Injection defense invokes pattern scanning on untrusted input channels.
Architectural boundary preventing reasoning modules from directly executing side effects.
Endpoint publishes a capability manifest declaring supported tools, resources, prompts, and extensions for discovery.
Endpoint requires or supports a specific authorization scheme for access control.
Endpoint emits a cancellation signal to abort an in-progress task or operation.
Endpoint exposes a parameterized prompt template for client-side materialization.
Endpoint exposes a readable or subscribable data resource identified by URI.
Endpoint exposes a callable tool with schema and invocation semantics accessible to protocol clients.
Participants engage in capability negotiation to agree on protocol version, extensions, and operational limits.
Completed or in-progress task yields a protocol artifact containing output data or files.
Endpoint accepts an inbound structured message envelope from a peer participant.
Endpoint returns a structured error envelope in response to a failed request or operation.
Endpoint transmits a structured message envelope to a peer participant.
Endpoint initiates a delegated task by submitting a task envelope to a remote executor.
Streaming channel is bound to an underlying transport mechanism for real-time delivery.
Endpoint reports a task lifecycle state transition on an in-flight delegated task.
Environment adapter specializes a provider adapter for execution environment differences.
Model adapter specializes a generic provider adapter for language model invocation.
Tool adapter specializes a generic provider adapter for external tool integration.
Protocol binding maps SDK concepts to external protocol primitives for interoperability.
Connector establishes connection to an external provider through a provider adapter.
SDK kernel emits structured runtime events conforming to the event model hierarchy.
SDK exposes a family of APIs providing unified access to agent capabilities and runtime operations.
SDK kernel classifies and propagates errors according to the structured error model.
SDK kernel implements the top-level agent SDK behavioral contracts for composition and provider swap.
SDK installs and activates a plugin package extending kernel behavior.
SDK loads a skill package bundling tools, prompts, and workflows into the active agent.
Plugin registers handlers at named extension points in the SDK kernel.
SDK persists and transports data using specified serialization formats.
Adapter performs actions on a connected environment on behalf of the agent.
Adapter specializes for a specific environment type category.
Environment exposes a permission surface defining allowed and denied operations.
Environment is associated with a quantified risk profile for policy evaluation.
Adapter observes state and events from a connected environment.
Adapter reads a captured or live environment state snapshot.
Adapter reports a normalized observation captured from the environment.
Action requires one or more permissions from the environment permission surface.
Adapter writes or updates environment state through controlled mutations.
Diagram alignment checker verifies ontology nodes align with architecture diagram references.
Release gate blocks ontology release when mandatory validation checks fail.
Coverage checker measures completeness of nodes and edges relative to expected ontology scope.
Required edge checker reports missing edges declared as mandatory on node class definitions.
Orphan node checker scans for disconnected nodes lacking required edge relationships.
Evidence checker validates provenance requirements on normative ontology artifacts.
Graph validator orchestrates execution of specialized sub-validators and checkers.

intra_subgraph_hierarchy.invariant: Each subgraph has one level-0 anchor; non-root nodes declare a same-subgraph parent at a lower level.
